dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive (University of Liverpool and Radboudumc)

pi_items.source: Web search for 'Dasatinib cancer drug indication' confirmed Dasatinib (SPRYCEL®) is used to treat Philadelphia chromosome-positive chronic myeloid leukemia (CML) and acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL)
Web search for 'Amiodarone antiarrhythmic drug' confirmed Amiodarone is a Class III antiarrhythmic medication used to treat and prevent cardiac arrhythmias
Confirmed in task.json and setup/export scripts

Web search of Liverpool drug interaction system documentation consistently shows RED, AMBER (not ORANGE), YELLOW, GREEN, GREY. Multiple sources confirm the traffic light system uses 'amber' not 'orange'

pi_items.verified_value: All keywords verified in documented Dasatinib-Amiodarone interaction
pi_items.source: Web search results from cancer-druginteractions.org, WebMD, and clinical literature confirm interaction mechanism involves: QT prolongation (QT, prolong), CYP3A4 inhibition (CYP, inhibit), hepatic metabolism (metabolism), increased exposure/concentrations (concentration), risk of Torsade de Pointes and cardiac arrhythmias (risk, arrhythmia)

pi_items.verified_value: Dasatinib-Amiodarone interaction is a documented, real interaction
pi_items.source: Cancer Drug Interactions from Radboud UMC and University of Liverpool (cancer-druginteractions.org), WebMD drug interactions, and clinical publications (Blood journal, American Heart Association) all document this interaction with recommendation for close monitoring

pi_items.verified_value: Pharmacokinetic (dasatinib weak CYP3A4 inhibitor increases amiodarone exposure) AND pharmacodynamic (additive QT prolongation risk, both drugs affect cardiac interval)
pi_items.source: Clinical literature and cancer-druginteractions.org identify dual mechanism: dasatinib increases amiodarone levels via CYP3A4 inhibition, plus both drugs independently prolong QT interval creating additive cardiac risk

pi_items.verified_value: Real data from Liverpool Drug Interactions Group (University of Liverpool and Radboudumc)
pi_items.source: Environment setup script references cancer-druginteractions.org; evidence documentation (ibrutinib_comedication_contraindication_screen_evidence.json line 78) explicitly states 'App contains real drug interaction data from Liverpool Drug Interactions Group'

privileged_info_summary: Dasatinib is a real cancer drug used for chronic myeloid leukemia and acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Amiodarone is a real antiarrhythmic drug. The documented interaction between them involves two mechanisms: dasatinib weakly inhibits CYP3A4, increasing amiodarone exposure and concentration, while both drugs independently prolong the QT interval, creating additive cardiac risk requiring close ECG monitoring. The app uses real data from the Liverpool Drug Interactions Group (University of Liverpool and Radboudumc). The required keywords (QT, prolong, CYP, metabolism, concentration, inhibit, risk, arrhythmia) are all present in documented descriptions of this interaction. The Liverpool traffic light color system uses AMBER (not ORANGE as claimed) for moderate risk interactions, along with RED, YELLOW, GREEN, and GREY.
